title: Final Project: Data Structure Application
summary: Build a small Python application that uses course data structures, includes tests, and explains design and complexity decisions.
studentInstructions: Create a small Python application that solves a realistic problem using at least one course data structure such as a stack, queue, linked list, tree, or traversal algorithm.
Suggested project ideas include a task scheduler, playlist manager, browser history simulator, expression tree evaluator, contact organizer, file tree explorer, or help desk queue simulator.
Create app.py as the main entry point and place supporting classes in structures.py or another clearly named Python file.
Create tests.py with tests for core behavior and important edge cases.
Create project_report.md explaining the problem, data structures used, class design, complexity of key operations, limitations, and possible improvements.
The project must be runnable from app.py without requiring external packages beyond the Python standard library unless clearly documented.

rubric.description: The application runs, solves a clear problem, and supports meaningful user or programmatic interactions without major crashes.
The project uses at least one course data structure appropriately and the choice fits the problem requirements.
The code is organized into clear classes or modules with focused responsibilities and readable interfaces.
The tests cover core features, edge cases, and at least one failure or boundary condition.
The report accurately explains key operation complexity, design decisions, limitations, and tradeoffs.
The code uses clear names, avoids unnecessary duplication, and is understandable to another student at the same level.

gradingInstructions: Use only this assignment and its rubric.
Check required files before grading quality.
